review_notes: The functions provided are mostly accurate and align well with the control CC6.8, which focuses on preventing unauthorized or malicious software. The function names follow the naming conventions and are descriptive. However, to enhance clarity, it might be beneficial to include additional functions that address other aspects of unauthorized software introduction, such as RBAC controls or secret management.
title: CC6.8 The entity implements controls to prevent or detect and act upon the introduction of unauthorized or malicious software to meet the entity’s objectives
step1_reasoning: The control CC6.8 focuses on preventing or detecting unauthorized or malicious software. In Kubernetes, this can be addressed through several mechanisms: ensuring that container images are scanned for vulnerabilities (k8s_image_vulnerability_scanning_enabled), enforcing security policies at the admission controller level to prevent the deployment of insecure pods (k8s_admission_controller_pod_security_enabled), applying network policies to restrict ingress traffic (k8s_networkpolicy_default_deny_ingress), and configuring pod security contexts to prevent running as root (k8s_pod_security_context_non_root). These controls collectively help in mitigating the risk of introducing malicious software into the Kubernetes environment.
